digest:
_Analyst: groq:openai/gpt-oss-120b · Red-team: gemini:gemini-3.5-flash (cross-model adversarial review)_

## Analyst
- **MP 8‑K (2026‑06‑10, Item 5.07)** – announced a $1.2 bn joint‑venture to lift Dy/Tb separation capacity, directly expanding the “heavy‑RE” (rare‑earth‑magnet) supply chain. With MP now “crowded” (+64% YTD, crowding 35) the new plant cuts scarcity risk, pulling the exit trigger forward.  

- **EME 8‑K (2026‑06‑04, Item 5.07)** – disclosed a 45% jump in contracted electrician/EPC work for hyperscale data‑centers, extending its backlog to ≈ 3 yr. This reinforces the “electricians” scarcity (EME YTD +33%, crowding 56) and strengthens the near‑term deploy case.  

- **CEG 8‑K (2026‑06‑02, “Other material event”)** – signed a 30 MW power‑purchase agreement with a new IPP, adding volume to the “electrons” scarcity (CEG YTD +48%, crowding 61). The added pipeline supports the already high‑priced power‑merchant niche, keeping a deployment bias.  

- **MU 8‑K (2026‑06‑09, Exec/board change)** – added a former Samsung DRAM veteran to the board, signaling tighter execution on HBM/TSV roll‑out. MU’s stock is up > 200% YTD (crowding 96) yet forward P/E remains N/A (effectively low), so valuation stays attractive despite the rally – buy‑on‑momentum still viable.  

- **Transformer squeeze (news 2026‑05‑11 & 2026‑06‑16)** – lead times now ≈ 4 yr, tightening the “large‑transformer” scarcity. ETN (YTD +27%, crowding 94) and GEV (YTD +48%, crowding 69) sit in this bucket; no filing yet, but the supply gap should lift pricing and make a FY‑24 guidance upgrade a near‑term entry catalyst.  

- **Gas‑turbine slot narrative (news 2026‑05‑28 & 2026‑06‑11)** – GE Vernova says turbines aren’t gating data‑center builds, softening the “turbines” scarcity pressure on GEV. With GEV already “crowded” (+48% YTD) the upside from turbine constraints is muted, tempering the short‑term upside trigger.  

- **Uranium price surge (headline 2026‑06‑10)** – contract prices at 14‑year highs keep the “uranium” scarcity (CCJ, U.UN) “crowded.” No recent filing, but if forward multiples stay modest the pricing premium could justify fresh long positions.  

- **Copper structural deficit (headlines 2026‑04‑16, 2026‑05‑19)** – persistent supply gap sustains “high‑priced” status for FCX, COPX, TECK, IVN (YTD +37‑41%, crowding 75‑85). Absence of new capacity announcements raises downside risk of price correction, suggesting a more defensive stance.  

- **Advanced‑packaging (ASML, TSM)** – both remain “high‑priced” (ASML YTD +59%, crowding 93). No SEC update, but strong CoWoS demand keeps the deployment trigger intact.  

- **Gallium supply shift (news 2025‑11‑12)** – China’s temporary suspension of its export ban on Ga/Ge could ease the “gallium” scarcity affecting WOLF, NVTS, QRVO. With these stocks still “medium‑priced” and no fresh filings, watch for a pricing reset that may bring an exit trigger closer.  

## Red-team
Here is a skeptical red-team teardown of the analyst digest:

*   **Immaterial "Scarcity" Claims (CEG):** The analyst cites a 30 MW power-purchase agreement (PPA) to support a deployment bias on Constellation Energy (CEG). Constellation has a generating capacity of over 32,000 MW. A 30 MW agreement represents less than **0.1% of its capacity**—it is a rounding error, entirely immaterial, and cannot support a thesis on "electrons scarcity" for a stock up 48% YTD. 
*   **Fundamental Misunderstanding of SEC Filings (MP & EME):** The analyst references **Item 5.07** filings (which are strictly for recording shareholder voting results at annual/special meetings) to claim new disclosures of a $1.2B joint venture (MP) and a 45% jump in contracted backlog (EME). These material operational developments would have been disclosed months prior in Item 1.01 or 2.02 filings. If these details are just now appearing in a 5.07 voting record, they are old news and 100% priced in.
*   **Bizarre Valuation Logic (MU):** The analyst claims Micron (MU) has an "attractive valuation" because its forward P/E is "N/A (effectively low)" despite the stock being up >200% YTD. In financial modeling, a "N/A" forward P/E means the company has negative projected earnings or highly volatile consensus estimates, making it high-risk—not "effectively low." Furthermore, assuming a single board seat addition will solve complex HBM/TSV packaging yield issues is extreme over-extrapolation.
*   **Severe Temporal Inconsistency (Transformer Squeeze):** Writing in **mid-2026** (per the news dates of May/June 2026), the analyst claims that transformer supply gaps will drive a **"FY-24 guidance upgrade"** as a near-term entry catalyst for ETN and GEV. FY24 financial results would have been finalized and reported nearly 18 months prior. 
*   **Inverted Commodity Logic (Copper):** The analyst argues that an "absence of new capacity announcements raises downside risk of price correction" for copper. In commodity markets, a lack of new supply capacity is highly *bullish* for prices because it exacerbates structural deficits. New capacity announcements would actually be the catalyst for a downward price correction.
*   **Stale, Outdated Catalyst (Gallium):** The analyst suggests watching for a "near-term pricing reset" based on China suspending its gallium/germanium export ban. However, the cited news is from **November 2025**—more than seven months prior to this June 2026 digest. Any market pricing reset from that policy change occurred half a year ago.
